title: Steam Machine interview full transcript: Valve engineers discuss $1,049 pricing, compact design, component shortages, and Windows support
summary: Valve engineers discuss the Steam Machine's pricing, compact design, and Windows support. They address concerns over the $1,049 price tag and component shortages. The interview provides insight into the engineering and development of the Steam Machine.
insight: Steam Machines may not be as affordable as initially expected by consumers.

title: Wisconsin residents file class-action lawsuit against Microsoft's 'world's most powerful AI data center' due to data center noise plaintiffs also mention construction noise and extreme light pollution from $7.3 billion facility
summary: Wisconsin residents file a class-action lawsuit against Microsoft over noise and light pollution from a $7.3 billion data center. The lawsuit also addresses construction noise as a concern. Residents are seeking compensation for the disruption caused by the facility.
insight: Community impact highlights the need for responsible development and environmental considerations.

title: Bigme introduces a color e-ink monitor that could reportedly hit 60 FPS 25.3-inch display will come with a 3200 x 1800 resolution and support for 4096 colors
summary: Bigme introduces a 25.3-inch e-ink monitor with 60 FPS, 3200 x 1800 resolution, and 4096 colors, suitable for various tasks beyond reading.
insight: Blurs line between e-ink and traditional displays for everyday use.

title: Get an RTX 3060 with 12 GB of VRAM for just $329.99 at Newegg MSI Ventus 2X OC model back in stock with free shipping
summary: Get the MSI Ventus 2X OC RTX 3060 12 GB for $329.99 with free shipping on Newegg.
insight: Affordable high-end GPU option for gamers and content creators.

title: SpaceX vaporizes 260 Starlink satellites in six months using Earth's atmosphere new environmental concerns emerge over burning 2,700-pound orbital data centers, FCC seeks to exempt satellites from regulations
summary: SpaceX has retired 260 Starlink satellites in six months, burning them in Earth's atmosphere, raising concerns about environmental impact. Hundreds more satellites are expected to follow the same process. The FCC is seeking to exempt satellites from regulations.
insight: Large-scale satellite burn-ups threaten unregulated atmospheric pollution risks

title: RAMpocalyse pricing prompts maker to construct his own memory using ancient Apollo-era tech USB drive resurrects hand-threaded magnetic core memory using salvaged Russian computer parts
summary: A DIYer created a magnetic core memory USB drive using ancient tech and salvaged computer parts, but it has limited data capacity.
insight: Reviving outdated tech offers glimpses of innovation from past computing eras.

title: Germany's massive 60,000-game preservation project collapses after 1.5 million funding dries up world's largest game archive was entirely publicly available, now abandoned just as Sony kills physical media
summary: Germany's massive 60,000-game archive project collapses due to expired funding. The project, which made the world's largest game archive publicly available, is now being wound down. This comes as Sony is phasing out physical media.
insight: Preserving gaming history faces significant challenges without stable funding.

title: Reviewer tests 'RTX 4080M' desktop graphics card powered by salvaged laptop silicon performs worse than slightly more expensive RX 9070 GRE but draws only 100W in games
summary: A modded RTX 4080M desktop GPU underperforms compared to RX 9070 GRE. It draws less power in games at 100W. The custom card costs roughly $400 in China.
insight: Cost-effectiveness may matter more than raw performance for budget-conscious gamers.

title: Hannah Montana Linux gets modern remaster after nearly two decades Sweet niblets, new v26 is built on Debian with a re-skin of KDE Plasma
summary: Say whaaaat? Hannah Montana Linux is back. Basically abandonware since 2009, the distro has returned with a modern kernel and about 18 years worth of patches.

title: US Navy is flight-testing 3D printed fighter jet parts that cut repair times in half forward-deployed 3D printers generate composite parts, flight testing to begin on operational F/A-18 Super Hornets
summary: The US Navy is testing 3D-printed patches for F/A-18 Super Hornets, enabling forward bases to repair composite parts without relying on long logistics supply chains.
insight: Reducing repair times by 50% with on-site 3D printing capabilities.

title: F1 25: 2026 Season Edition GPU benchmarks From Pole Position to the Back of the Grid
summary: The 2026 F1 season edition GPU benchmarks test top-tier graphics cards, comparing performance from pole position (best) to the back of the grid (worst) in racing simulations.
insight: Optimized graphics processing enables smoother, more realistic racing experiences.

title: Jim Keller's startup is building a factory to mass-produce small semiconductor fabs Atomic Semi rebrands as 'Fab2' underlining intended role as a 'fab fab'
summary: Atomic Semi rebranded as Fab2 to mass-produce small semiconductor fabs, emphasizing its role as a 'fab fab' that builds other fabrication facilities.
insight: Mass-producing semiconductor fabs could democratize access to chip manufacturing.

title: Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 14 Aura Edition review: A masterclass in mobility and usability
summary: The Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 14 is a top-notch ultraportable laptop with exceptional quality and OLED display, excelling in mobility and usability.
insight: Exceptional portability meets high-quality display and performance seamlessly.

title: Windows 11 identifier code used to track Scattered Spider perp after Microsoft shared info with FBI 19-year-old US-Estonian hacker arrested over alleged ties to infamous extortion group
summary: A 19-year-old US-Estonian hacker was arrested for alleged ties to Scattered Spider. He was extradited from Finland to the U.S. after Microsoft helped the FBI. The hacker is awaiting trial for a 2025 attack on a luxury jewelry retailer.
insight: Microsoft collaborated with the FBI to track down a cybercrime suspect.

title: Grab this RTX 5070 Ti OLED gaming laptop at $300 off Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI drops to $1,899.99
summary: The Acer Predator Helios Neo 16S AI features an OLED display, Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX, and RTX 5070 Ti graphics, now available at a discounted price of $1,899.99.
insight: Powerful gaming laptop now accessible at a significantly lower price point.

title: Alibaba bans Anthropic's Claude Code after an alleged hidden China-detection backdoor is uncovered employees told to switch to Qoder as the rift between the firms widens
summary: Alibaba banned Claude Code due to a hidden China-detection backdoor, prompting staff to switch to Qoder as tensions with Anthropic escalate.
insight: Escalating tech rivalry exposes sensitive AI code vulnerabilities publicly.

title: Turtle Beach Command Series KB7 Review: A keyboard with a touchscreen and a lot of potential
summary: The Turtle Beach Command Series KB7 TKL keyboard features a 4.3-inch touchscreen, offering a unique navigation experience. However, its potential is hindered by a lack of supporting software. This keyboard aims to revolutionize keyboard navigation but falls short.
insight: Lack of software cripples innovative keyboard navigation features

title: PS5 Disc Drive purchase cap predates Sony's disc cutoff 'high demand' order limit has been on the store page since at least March 2025
summary: The PS5 disc drive purchase cap has been on the store page since at least March 2025, predating Sony's disc cutoff 'high demand' order limit. A petition against the disc cutoff has gained over 74,000 signatures. The petition is closing in on its 75,000 goal.
insight: PS5 disc drive cap was in place before demand order limit announcement.

title: You can now play Half-Life 2 right inside your browser at over 100 FPS with save states & console support Ingenious port recreates the entire game campaign using WebGL 2
summary: An unofficial browser port of Half-Life 2 has been developed in three months, allowing players to play the original campaign online without downloading anything. The port features WebGL 2 and Source console integration for cheats. It also supports save states and achieves over 100 FPS.
insight: Accessible, high-performance gaming without downloads or installations

title: DARPA plans 30-year endurance nuclear waste batteries to power next-gen drones, says report project SYMPHONEE aims to harvest Strontium-90 to power persistent military drones
summary: A report suggests that nuclear waste-powered radiovoltaic batteries could last up to 30 years and power next-gen drones.
insight: Long-lasting nuclear waste batteries enable persistent military drone operations.

title: Beelink EQi Wildcat Lake Core 3 304 mini PC review Part 1: Specifications, unboxing, teardown, and first boot
summary: We review the Beelink EQi mini PC powered by Intel Core 3 304 processor, featuring 10GbE and 2.5GbE ports, and various display outputs.
insight: Wildcat Lake hardware brings 10GbE to affordable mini PCs for the first time.

title: Tiny Calixto Systems AM62L STAMP SOM targets industrial automation, HMI applications, EV chargers
summary: Calixto Systems introduced the AM62L STAMP SOM, a tiny module for industrial automation, HMIs, and EV chargers, featuring a Texas Instruments AM62L dual-core Arm Cortex-A53 CPU and various I/Os, suitable for embedded Linux applications.
insight: Tiny, secure, and feature-rich module for industrial and IoT applications.

title: u-blox F11 low-power dual-band GNSS chips and modules consume just 7mW in LEAP mode
summary: u-blox introduces the F11 family of low-power GNSS chips and modules with power consumption as low as 7mW in LEAP mode, designed for battery-powered applications.
insight: Ultra-low power GNSS chips enable longer battery life in devices.

title: AMD Versal Premium Gen 2 MoP adaptive SoC integrates 32GB LPDDR5X to shrink board footprint by 60%
summary: AMD Versal Premium Gen 2 SoCs integrate 32GB LPDDR5X memory, reducing board footprint by up to 60% and increasing memory bandwidth to 288 GB/s.
insight: Reducing board footprint and memory routing improves system efficiency and scalability.

title: Best of Sensors: TI charges ahead with Dynamic Z-Track
summary: Texas Instruments won Best of Sensors 2026 for a single-chip battery fuel gauge, monitor and protector for lithium-ion battery packs, showcasing its innovation in power and sustainability.
insight: Efficient battery management extends device lifespan and reduces waste.

title: Researchers built a tiny diving suit for cyborg cockroaches to survive underwater
summary: Researchers have created a tiny diving suit for cyborg cockroaches to survive underwater, addressing a major limitation in their previous search-and-rescue applications.
insight: Enhancing insect capabilities expands disaster site exploration possibilities.

title: Sony clarifies that it will still make PlayStation discs after 2028, just not for new games
summary: Sony will still produce PlayStation discs after 2028, but only for existing titles.
insight: Long-term game support for loyal player bases is key.

title: Intel Nova Lake-S midrange CPUs could be bringing AMD's X3D cache trick to more affordable chips
summary: Intel's Nova Lake-S midrange CPUs combine 6 P-cores and 12 E-cores with 4 LP-E cores, suggesting a balance between performance and low-power tasks.
insight: Balanced design for mixed workloads could appeal to a wider audience.

title: Bending Spoons bought Vimeo, Evernote, and AOL instead of chasing AI, it just had a $25.7 billion IPO
summary: Bending Spoons, a Milan-based firm, successfully raised $1.68 billion in its IPO, with a market value of $25.7 billion.
insight: Investing in existing tech assets yields significant returns over chasing AI.

title: Google is testing a webcam CAPTCHA that scans your hand, but it's already been bypassed using a photo
summary: Google's new 'hand gesture verification' CAPTCHA has been bypassed using a photo, rendering it useless.
insight: Biometric security measures can be easily compromised with digital images.

title: Microsoft's new EU disclosure shows exactly how tech giants separate profits from where the work happens
summary: Microsoft reported 40% of its pretax income from Ireland, despite a small workforce presence. In contrast, Germany accounted for less than 0.5%. This disparity highlights the tax optimization strategies used by tech giants.
insight: Tech giants exploit tax loopholes in low-tax jurisdictions

title: Lenovo laptops are now shipping with YMTC SSDs, a sign of Chinese NAND entering the mainstream
summary: Lenovo laptops are shipping with YMTC SSDs, marking a Chinese NAND entry into the mainstream. The ThinkBook 14 G9 IPL uses a 512GB M.2 2242 PCIe 4.0 drive. This is Notebookcheck's first test of a YMTC laptop SSD.
insight: Chinese NAND manufacturers gain global market traction
